texto: Denton struck Charley as the kind of man who never wasted energy on extra movement or idle chitchat. He was foursquare Sonny Boy Williamson and Sister Rosetta Tharpe, a Silvertone guitar, older than old school.
explicacao: Citação de Natalie Baszile sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Denton struck Charley as the kind of man who never wasted energy on extra movement or idle chitch…” articula reflexao, character, denton em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: Denton struck Charley as the kind of man who never wasted energy on extra movement or idle chitchat. He was foursquare Sonny Boy Williamson and Sister Rosetta Tharpe, a Silvertone guitar, older than old school.

texto: The priest motioned for the congrecation to sit, then welcomed everyone in the name of the Father, Son and Holy Spirit. He wasn't so much thin as soft, with wisps of dull brown hair and oversize glasses that made him look gooberish. As he shook holy water over a bundle of cane stalks leaning against the wall, Charley wondered if his blessing would be powerful enough, because from where she sat, it looked like he'd have trouble asking for extra mayo on his sandwich.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Natalie Baszile: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “The priest motioned for the congrecation to sit, then welcomed everyone in the name of the Father…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, character, priest pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: The priest motioned for the congrecation to sit, then welcomed everyone in the name of the Father, Son and Holy Spirit. He wasn't so much thin as soft, with wisps of dull brown hair and oversize glasses that made him look gooberish. As he shook holy water over a bundle of cane stalks leaning against the wall, Charley wondered if his blessing would be powerful enough, because from where she sat, it looked like he'd have trouble asking for extra mayo on his sandwich.

texto: Girl, you're free, can't you see that? You've got your child, you've got your family down here who love you, you've got your farm. You don't have to ask for anything. You know how few women in this world get to say that, black or white?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Natalie Baszile que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Girl, you're free, can't you see that? You've got your child, you've got your family down here wh…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, life-and-living, women, com foco em women.
